title: sarothra gymnantha
intro: Sarothra gymnantha, also known as the East Indian arrowroot or East Indian wood fern, is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a natural laxative. The health benefits of Sarothra gymnantha include its potential to treat digestive issues, lower blood pressure, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Sarothra gymnantha can be prepared as a tea, powder, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to treat a range of ailments.
